neighborhood_overview:

name: Canalhouse in the Center
description:
This beautiful canal house is a charming place conveniently located in the heart of Amsterdam’s Canal District near 'Het IJ'. The estuary where during the Golden Age, merchandise and spices were supplied and stored from all corners of the world.

The canal house is situated within walking distance of the main museums, popular shopping areas, and historic landmarks.

The house is just renovated and geüpgraded in January 2024.
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is located in the city center of Amsterdam. It only takes 5 minutes by foot to arrive to the Waterloopsquare.
It’s 15 minutes walking to the Red light district and the famous Rembrandtsquare. 20 minutes walking to the Central station.
At the Waterloopsquare and Rembrandtplein you find the nearest supermarkets, shops, pubs, coffeeshops and restaurants.
Also in the direct area of the house you can find some nice bars and restaurants.

The nearby Foeliestraat reminds us of the latter, a spice that has been loved since the Middle Ages and is better known as nutmeg. Walk with us through the area.

In the 19th century, the neighborhood was a refuge for Jews who had fled the pogroms in Eastern Europe. They had already been preceded by Sephardic Jews who had been expelled from Spain and Portugal since 1492. The Portuguese synagogue is still a reminder of them, which for a long time was considered the largest synagogue in the world.
host_about:
As a family business we run the quest house with passion and enthusiasm. 
Our goal is to provide our guests with warm and homely accommodation and let it be a home away from home.

neighborhood_overview:
'De Pijp' is Amsterdam's most vibrant neighborhood. Close to the Museum Quarter, 'De Pijp' gives you everything you look for in Amsterdam: small, cobbled streets, nice cafes, coffee corners and one of a kind restaurants and shops. Whether you stroll down the streets or sit down on one of the many terraces, visiting 'De Pijp' shows you what Amsterdam is all about. This is the hotspot where all new things in Amsterdam are happening.

I love walking along the Albert Cuyp Market or heading over to Sarphatipark. Grab some homemade pancakes at Bakers & Roasters around the corner or a coffee at Coffee & Coconuts (located in a converted old theater). One of my favorite restaurants is conveniently located in the same street as my apartment. The tiny Indonesian restaurant Spang Makandra also does take away (in case you're intimidated by the line outside). You could of course try your luck and wait for a seat inside. neighborhood_overview:
You’ll be staying in the vibrant Oud-West district, an up-and-coming area with a growing array of activities and services in recent years. Most buildings were constructed in the early 20th century, and today it’s one of the city’s most bohemian neighborhoods, with designer shops, brunch spots, and specialty coffee houses, as well as a wide range of entertainment options.

Leidseplein, just 350 meters from the hotel, is the nightlife hub of the neighborhood, with restaurants, bars, nightclubs, cinemas, and theaters. This area of Amsterdam also features artisan and gourmet food markets at the cultural space De Hallen. Not to mention that the city’s green lung, Vondelpark, is just 300 meters away, offering 47 hectares, including the Openluchttheater, an open-air theater hosting free performances every summer.

neighborhood_overview:
The Neighbourhood
Amsterdam East is one of the upcoming so called hipster neighbourhoods in Amsterdam! It almost seems that a new hotspot pops up almost every day in this neighbourhood. It’s nearby the city centre, but has such a different vibe. A drink at the rooftop of The Volkshotel is a must to enjoy the stunning views over Amsterdam! When it comes to restaurants there is something for everyone. From trendy coffee bars, industrial warehouses, cocktail bars to concept stores! The Czaar Peter Street and the Javastraat are some of the best shopping streets on the East side of town. All kinds of trendy shops can be found here. On a sunny day it’s definitely a must to visit Roest for a drink on the waterfront. If you want the local feel and multi-culti then stroll over the
Dappermarket or wander around the Oosterpark. The renewed Beukenplein is perfect for a drink afterwards!
host_about: My name is Myriam and I live with my partner and two daughters in Amsterdam. I have my own business. In my work I meet a lot of people with different cultural backgrounds. My mission is to contribute to a better world in which respect, compassion and tolerance are important keywords. Together with my eldest daughter Liza, who studies at the UVA (university of Amsterdam) I started this RB&B in my working place. We look forward to meet you in our very nice apartment.

A beautiful park in the middle of the city was created - and still is today. Large trees that provide shade in summer and absorb the sounds of the city.
I love the Cafe Slijterij Oosterling (50m from us). Open since 1877, this is probably one of the most authentic places in Amsterdam. Time seems to stand still here. description: Wake up to a view of a 16th century Dutch windmill, nestled close to local cafés in hip west Amsterdam. Take a leisurely 20-minute stroll to the popular 9 Streets for shopping nirvana or stay closer to home for an expert espresso and a delicious local pastry. neighborhood_overview:
Pop into any of the local fashion stores around the neighbourhood, pet cats at the cat café around the corner and try some delicious traditional pancakes at MOOK afterwards. The artistic and musical venue De Marktkantine (Market Canteen), is just around the corner. Check out our city guide for more recommendations!

This bridge house is classed as NOISE LEVEL - MEDIUM.
host_about:
SWEETS hotel is a one-of-a-kind hotel located across Amsterdam in 28 different bridge houses. Each bridge house has been transformed into an independent hotel suite that accommodates a maximum of two guests.

For centuries, Amsterdam’s bridge houses accommodated the city’s many bridge keepers who were responsible for opening the impressive structures for passing water traffic. With building dates between 1673 and 2009, the bridge houses carry an extensive history. Visiting them in chronological order is like a journey through time: their various architectural styles reveal how the city developed and grew, when new quarters were built and even how people thought about city development at that time.

With the hotel room interiors, SWEETS hotel offers guests a unique and comfortable living space, while keeping the history of every bridge house in mind. All bridge houses are different, and so are the interiors. Every SWEET accommodates up to two adults and contains a kingsize double bed, a bathroom and coffee and tea facilities.

SWEETS hotel, first initiated in 2012 as an urban space project, is 8 years in the making. Over half of the 28 bridge houses are currently available for reservations, with more coming soon.

SWEETS hotel is an initiative and co-creation of the architecture office Space&Matter (known for De Ceuvel, an ecological hub), project development partner Grayfield and Suzanne Oxenaar and Otto Nan of Seven New Things, founders of Lloyd Hotel & Cultural Embassy (the world’s first 1- to 5-star hotel) and Hotel The Exchange.

host_about:
Hello,
We love AIrbnb. It is a great concept. We are proud te be a member of this community and love to explore the world at other Airbnb nice places. We introduce ourselves.
We arrived in Amsterdam in 1992 ( Michiel) and 1994 (Marlous) for doing our study and since then we didn’t leave this amazing city (except all the travels and work experiences abroad) so we discovered the city at early age ( with experiencing all the good and bad things), started our career over here and now we have our family life in this lovely neighborhood which feels like a modern upgraded ghetto in the middle of the energetic city of Amsterdam.

We enjoy our family life with the kids who goes to school and has there friends nearby and playgrounds are all over around (our garden, “Vondelpark”, “Museumplein”) and within a second we leave the house for a movie (at Cinecenter Cinema near "Leidseplein" is our favorite) or a theatre (Stadsschouwburg at "Leidseplein" which also has lots of International Plays) or having drinks with friends in one of the bars or restaurant nearby (the “Pijp” or the Jordaan”). All within a short distance or a 10 or 20 minute walk. 

We love to work hard as an Photographer and an Advocate and doing sports like racecycling, jogging, yoga and we all play tennis. We do love art and painting. We loved to travel around the world and now with our kids we downsized the distance to overcome and explore Europe with his nice countries like Italia (our favorite till so far is the lovely place Lucca) and Austria (for skieing and staying in a nice family village like Zell am See), Turkey (with the charming surfing hotspot of Alacati) and our favorite cities like Paris ( we love to eat a croissant with our kids in Parque Luxembourg) and Tarifa (for hanging around in this kite-surfing place paradise). We also enjoy simply going to the nearby beaches of Bloemendaal (Beachclub Republiek), Zandvoort ( Family Beach Vooges) or Noordwijk (Beachclub Zuiderbad) and we love to overstay a weekend in our familyhouse in Zeeland. We enjoying life with our friends and being with our lovely kids.

We are also interested in Houseswap with another family for a nice place which is child friendly, has 5 beds available, situated nearby sea and beach or nice cities in Europa, a lovely apartment in New York or anything special and authentique. Don't hesistate to contact us!

description: We were able to build our dream. A unique, modern and comfortable houseboat of 35 x 6 meters. neighborhood_overview:
The Amsterdam Canal District is a neighborhood in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The seventeenth-century canals of Amsterdam, located in the center of Amsterdam, were added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in August 2010. The Amsterdam Canal District consists of the area around the city’s four main canals: Singel, Herengracht, Keizersgracht, and Prinsengracht. From Brouwersgracht, the canals are generally parallel with one another, leading gradually southeast into the Amstel river.

Many of the canal houses in the Amsterdam Canal District are from the Dutch Golden Age in the 18th century. Many of these buildings, however, underwent restoration or reconstruction in various centuries, meaning that these building display many different architectural styles and facades.

De Pijp is renowned for its sparkling atmosphere and lively nightlife. Experience the coziness of local bars, and enjoy live music in intimate cafes. Or relax with a drink on one of the many terraces and enjoy the lively ambiance of this unique neighborhood.

Stroll through the lively streets and discover an abundance of boutiques, cozy cafes, trendy bars and international restaurants. Enjoy the diverse kitchens and taste delicious local delicacies at the famous Albert Cuyp market, where you can find fresh produce and unique souvenirs.

neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam Oud-Zuid is one of the most desirable residential neighbourhoods with fancy shopping streets, world-famous museums and a beautiful city park.

Highlights
The prosperous city district of Oud-Zuid (Old South), part of the borough Zuid, has the city's most fashionable shopping streets: PC Hooftstraat, Van Baerlestraat and Beethovenstraat. Don't miss Cornelis Schuytstraat and its trendy boutiques and home stores for a special Dutch outfit or gift.

Top attractions
On Museumplein (Museum Quarter), which belongs to Oud-Zuid, you'll find all top museums close to each other: Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, Stedelijk Museum, Moco Museum and Diamond Museum.

Other big attractions in this posh district are the Vondelpark and Concertgebouw.

Amsterdam-Noord is a vibrant and increasingly popular neighborhood known for its creative atmosphere and diverse cultural attractions. Situated on the northern bank of the IJ River, this neighborhood offers a blend of historical charm and modern amenities. Here are some features of Amsterdam-Noord:

1. Culture and Art: The neighborhood is home to several cultural hotspots, including the renovated NDSM wharf, now a creative hub with art installations, studios, and restaurants. The EYE Filmmuseum, located by the water, attracts film enthusiasts and offers stunning views of the city.

2. Natural Beauty: Amsterdam-Noord is surrounded by greenery and water. The Vliegenbos is Amsterdam's oldest city forest, offering walking and recreational opportunities. Along the waterfront, you'll find cozy spots to relax and enjoy views of the city, such as the Noorderpark and Tolhuistuin.

name: Ruime gezinswoning met tuin
description:
Wonderful spacious and cozy family home in an ideal location. Quiet and "hidden" among the greenery, but close to the hustle and bustle of the city. The house has a spacious living room with sliding doors to the sunny garden. Here is seating, a dining table, bbq and a tropical lounge cabin. Upstairs are three large bedrooms (1 with balcony) with large double beds.
The Amsterdam Forest is just around the corner. Amenities and Zuidas at walking distance. Perfect tram connection to center.
neighborhood_overview:
Centrally located in a quiet, child-friendly residential area, at the edge of the Amsterdamse Bos, close to the Rai, the Zuidas and Amsterdam Zuid.

Within walking distance is a cozy little square with shops, restaurants and a supermarket.
There are excellent and fast public transport connections to downtown Amsterdam. The cozy city center of Amstelveen is also located in the nearby area. Tram stops within walking distance. Highway easily accessible.

Our house is hidden between the countryside, with the nature reserve Het Groene Lint right behind us. There is no traffic at the front door. The garden is located to the south and is very sunny and green. Lots of privacy! Between the apple and pear trees, you can go barbecuing. The garden offers a variety of lounge facilities and a tropical cabin where you can chill out.

We live in our house. If you like "sleek", this might not be the best choice. Those who love coziness will not be disappointed.

The area offers a unique mix of cultural vibrancy, local charm, and a peaceful retreat from the tourist-heavy center while remaining well-connected to the rest of Amsterdam.

Why Amsterdam Oost?
Amsterdam Oost is an increasingly popular district that perfectly blends the city’s rich history with modern innovation.

It’s a multicultural, trendy neighborhood brimming with lush green spaces, fascinating museums, diverse cuisine, and a laid-back vibe.

While central Amsterdam can sometimes feel overcrowded, Oost provides a quieter and more authentic experience, making it an excellent base for exploring the city.

Highlights of Amsterdam Oost

1. Proximity to the City Center
Amsterdam Oost is just a short tram or bike ride away from the city center. With excellent public transport connections, you can easily visit iconic attractions like the Anne Frank House, Rijksmuseum, or Van Gogh Museum, all within 15–30 minutes.

neighborhood_overview:
The Pijp, located near the center of Amsterdam, is known for its lively atmosphere, diverse cultural mix, and bustling streets. This neighborhood, originally built in the late 19th century, has a rich history and has evolved into one of the city's trendiest and most cosmopolitan districts.

Characteristic of De Pijp are its narrow streets, historic buildings, and vibrant markets, such as the famous Albert Cuyp Market, where you can find a variety of fresh produce, clothing, flowers, and exotic delicacies. The atmosphere is further enhanced by the many cozy cafes, trendy restaurants, and chic boutiques scattered along the streets.

The neighborhood is also known for its diverse population, with a mix of local residents, expatriates, and students contributing to the cosmopolitan atmosphere. This is reflected in the diverse culinary scene, ranging from traditional Dutch eateries to international cuisines from all corners of the world.

There are two toilets, two bedrooms, a big and sunny garden, private parking and it's very kids friendly.
neighborhood_overview:
The centre of Amsterdam is very close. From our house it's 12 minutes cycling to the ferry, which takes you to Central station in minutes. The ferry is free and runs 24/7. From there, all the landmarks (Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh, Anne Frank, Rembrandt) are nearby. We have two adult bikes (both with child seats, both have back-pedal brakes) at your disposal. Or take the bus: in 25 minutes you’re in the heart of Amsterdam. (You could take a car, but we never do. Amsterdam is made for bikes).
If you head in the other direction, there are several important landmarks quite close: Alkmaar, Zaanse Schans, Marken, Zandvoort. The beach is 30 minutes by car, and closer you will find natural swimming pools in nature park ‘Het Twiske’, almost in our back yard ;-).
host_about:
Creativity is important in our family. Erik is a creative director at Apple. Roos is a photographer, specialised in personal portraits (you can find her work on Insta: @roosdebolsterphotography). 
Our daughter Fiene creates the most beautiful drawings (at least we think so). We are kind and caring people who appreciate the finer things in life in good company.

neighborhood_overview:
Amsterdam's Jordaan is probably the most famous neighbourhood in the Netherlands.
The Jordaan is nothing but highlights. Maze-like streets offer hidden courtyards, cafés (traditional but also trendy), art galleries and beautiful canals and canal houses. One also has access to some of the more scenic outdoor markets in the country. In particular, Noordermarkt-Westerstraat has a flea market on Monday mornings and an organic farmers' market at The lindengracht on Saturdays. There are many more modern,
trendy bars around Noordermarkt and along Westerstraat and Rozengracht. The side-streets that run south of Westerstraat have recently developed into a strong culinary district with some of the city’s more highly regarded restaurants.

Furthermore you will find many city-hotspots within walking distance like the Anne-Frank museum, Dam square and the 9 streets.

description: Enjoy full privacy in this charming city apartment (it's not just a room!) Welcome to this cosy 60-square-meter space with an industrial vibe, with typical Dutch touches and vintage items. Perfect for 2-4 guests. It includes a bedroom for two, a spacious living room with a sofa bed (for two), and a bathroom with a shower and toilet. neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is in a quiet street and literally next to the house is a little park with a playground and a children's farm - very unique in Amsterdam! Also very nearby is the Bellamyplein, a cosy small square which in summer is a favourite hang out for parents with small children because of a little pool.

In our neighbourhood (Old West) are a lot of good restaurants and coffee bars, for example in our street the delicious Italian restaurant "Spaghetteria". At the end of the street starts the authentic Ten Kate Market. The newest addition to the area's hip dining scene is "De Hallen Amsterdam". A centre for culture, food, fashion and crafts which recently won the Geurt Brinkgreve Bokaal 2014, a prestigious price for architecture and heritage. Within a 5 minute walk you enter "De Hallen" with a movie theatre, gallery, library, Local Goods Store, bicycle store, and so on, and the great "Foodhallen" where you can enjoy a large variety of delicious bites and drinks.

name: Spacious apartment with garden | City Centre
description: This is a great apartment for your stay in Amsterdam. It's a spacious two story ground floor apartment with view at Jordaan and the Westertoren. The first floor offers a spacious living and dining room. The kitchen is fully equipped with all the modern equipment to make a delicious meal. Downstairs you find two separated bedrooms, a modern bathroom including a bath and a separate toilet. There is even a place where you can put your laundry. I am sure you will have a great time here. Enjoy!
neighborhood_overview:
This wonderful residence is situated close to trendy bars and restaurants in the Jordaan area, the main shopping area (Negen Straatjes, Kalverstraat and PC Hooftstraat). A very relaxing and spacious apartment right in the booming city centre.

By bike or tram you can reach some of the best museums in the world within 10 minutes: Rijksmuseum, Stedelijk Museum, Van Gogh Museum...

The more sportive types can easily do pick a workout at the Marnixbad (pool and gym). And for a refreshing walk you have Westerpark and Vondelpark around the corner.

Want to relax like a real local does? Fill up the cool box, rent a 'sloep' (longboat) and enjoy the canals by cruising around. At 100 meters you find a company that rents 'sloepen.

Just around the corner you find all kinds of shops for your daily groceries: supermarkets, fresh bakery, butcher, etc. On Saturday the close by Lindengracht market and biological Noordermarkt are worth a visit.
host_about:
Adam Suites is taking care of my apartment while I am gone.

Nice to meet you!

We are a family with a nine year old boy, two daughters in university and a dog called Bambu. Caspar is a busy dude that loves his work as an independent consultant but also is a wonderful cook. He loves skiing in the winter and lazy reading in summertime. Matilde - in The Netherlands called Mechteld - has a knack for languages and works in communications and visibility. She has a great passion for horses: classical dressage preferably.

The beauty of Amsterdam to us is the fact that each district has its own character. Whether you go to the Jordaan, Westerpark, Grachtengordel, Zuid, De Pijp or Oost, every visit you will be surprised with the typical individual atmosphere. Just like our friends, everyone is unique.

Would you like to know what we miss most of Amsterdam when we are away? Cycling all over town and of course a wholegrain sandwich with Beemster cheese from the Lindengracht market. There is nothing better in the whole wide world!

Meanwhile our motto - Carpe Diem and sometimes Carpe Noctem too -  can also be applied to the city we live in. Daylife and nightlife is exiting, inspiring and not comparable with any other capital. Except for a bunch of great restaurants, the so called 'Venice of the North' offers you nothing Italian!

We are enthusiastic to to help you enjoying our city to make the most of your stay. So do not hesitate to contact us for further information!

With its year-round 19,5° average, this charming Spanish villa on the first floor makes for a fantastic holiday destination.

Room features: Shared Bathroom
neighborhood_overview: Traditionally a blue collar neighbourhood just a stone's throw away from the heart of town, 'De Baarsjes', is what one calls 'on the rise'. All around us we see new initiatives popping up; from intimate coffee bars and restaurants, to cool boutiques with local designers. Please ask the staff for inside info on where to go!
host_about:
Hotel Not Hotel Amsterdam is a place where a room isn’t just a room, but a work of art with its own creator, story, and identity. Most of them designed and specially made by Collaboration-O, a group of young designers that originated from the Eindhoven Design Academy, and one by Arno Coenen and his partner IRIS.

When you wake up, you step into an art-filled, exhibition-like space instead of an anonymous lobby or hallway. The entire hotel is one big living room, with cosy seating areas between the cottages. Combined with our luxury beds and other facilities, Hotel Not Hotel offers an unrivalled experience. 
Besides all this, we feel that the value of a city lies in experiencing its local day-to-day life. As we are closely connected to Amsterdam and its numerous creative entrepreneurs, we know where to send you for a night on the town, a hidden culinary gem, or an upcoming artist’s exhibition. 

The bar connected to Hotel Not Hotel is called Kevin Bacon. Just like the actor, the bar has zero pretention, it always delivers, and it makes sure everybody has a good time.

Kevin Bacon is not your typical hotel bar. It’s where locals get their morning coffee or a quick breakfast. It’s where friends meet for a cocktail or a beer before diving into the nightlife, and where hotel guests let their hair down after a long day in the city.

When the weather is good, the ‘Whitey the White’ terrace is sunny like a perfectly fried egg. The staff’s friendly attitude shines all year round.

name: Nice Room near to RAI and downtown
description:
This one room is located in the Rivierenbuurt, a peaceful neighbourhood about 5 minutes walk from the RAI.

All the amenities are in the room for a comfortable and private stay: bed, private bathroom with shower, wifi, etc... There is also a waterboiler to make some coffee or tea.

Like most apartments in Amsterdam, there are stairs to climb. :-) Also, it is important that check-in take place before 9 pm.
neighborhood_overview: The Rivierenbuurt (Rivers Neighborhood) is one of the most pleasant in Amsterdam. Very peaceful, near to both nature (the Amstel River and plenty of local parks) and the city (closest to the Pijp). Both the Museumplein and Downtown are 10 minutes away with the bicycle or by tram (with a stop close by). The famous Albert Cuyp market is within walking distance and there are plenty of shops of all kinds, including all the basics, also within 1 to 2 minutes walk.

The Superstay is a Boutique Guestroom (completely private) located in the bustling heart of Quartier Latin, right in Amsterdam, with the Museumplein around the corner.
All major tourist attractions are close to the Superstay, such as the Heineken Experience, boat trips, the Albert Cuijp market, and the Museum Square. The beautiful canals are a short walk away. Vondelpark and Leidseplein are 10 min walk.

The area where you stay is very vibrant, multicultural and full of restaurants, pubs and sunny terraces. Behind the Albert Cuijpmarkt is a beautiful park where you can walk or picnic, as the locals do.
In the side streets of the Ferdinand Bolstraat, 100 meters away from the Superstay, you will find beautiful boutiques and fashion shops.

description: Four people can live comfortably in this mezzanine apartment with views of the garden or canal, plus exclusive extras.
neighborhood_overview:
Neighbourhood: Plantage
A short cycle east of the city centre, Nieuwe Kerkstraat sits within the desirable Plantage district. Famous for its elegant squares and leafy boulevards, Amsterdam’s ‘cultural garden’ is packed with theatres, galleries, museums and parks, yet conveniently close to the thriving business district.
host_about: Wittenberg by Cove is a monumental short stay building, offering modern accommodation in Amsterdam's residential area. Carre Theater and the Dutch National Opera & Ballet are just 500 meters from the property. All other city highlights are easily accessible by pubic transport. All apartments are fully equipped, including flat-screen TVs (Smart HD TV), dishwasher, oven, and microwave. Each has its own bathroom with free toiletries and a hairdryer. For your comfort, towels, bed linen and a weekly housekeeping are provided. Some apartments also feature a washing machine and there are public laundry rooms. The Hermitage Museum is just 650 meters away, while Artis Zoo is only 400 meters from Wittenberg by Cove. The nearest airport is Schiphol Airport, 12 km away or a 30-minute drive by car.

name: 207 - Nieuwe Amstelbrug
description: A cosy bridge house on the Amstel river in a vibrant neighbourhood near the popular Albert Cuyp market. The interior is divided into two floors with bathroom facilities on the lower level and a playful mezzanine bedroom above. Stroll to local cafés in the fashionable neighbourhood of De Pijp or take a walk into the centre of Amsterdam along the Amstel river (25 minutes).
neighborhood_overview:
Bridge house Nieuwe Amstelbrug is situated just outside the city centre, adjacent to neighbourhood 'De Pijp', one of the most popular areas in Amsterdam. Take a stroll on the famous Albert Cuypmarkt in the morning, grab some lunch at the end and visit the museums around Museum Square with renewed energy. Don't feel like walking? Hop on the tram, train or bus – all around the corner of Nieuwe Amstelbrug. Check out our city guide for more recommendations!

This bridge house is classed as NOISE LEVEL - MEDIUM.
host_about:
SWEETS hotel is a one-of-a-kind hotel located across Amsterdam in 28 different bridge houses. Each bridge house has been transformed into an independent hotel suite that accommodates a maximum of two guests.

For centuries, Amsterdam’s bridge houses accommodated the city’s many bridge keepers who were responsible for opening the impressive structures for passing water traffic. With building dates between 1673 and 2009, the bridge houses carry an extensive history. Visiting them in chronological order is like a journey through time: their various architectural styles reveal how the city developed and grew, when new quarters were built and even how people thought about city development at that time.

With the hotel room interiors, SWEETS hotel offers guests a unique and comfortable living space, while keeping the history of every bridge house in mind. All bridge houses are different, and so are the interiors. Every SWEET accommodates up to two adults and contains a kingsize double bed, a bathroom and coffee and tea facilities.

SWEETS hotel, first initiated in 2012 as an urban space project, is 8 years in the making. Over half of the 28 bridge houses are currently available for reservations, with more coming soon.

SWEETS hotel is an initiative and co-creation of the architecture office Space&Matter (known for De Ceuvel, an ecological hub), project development partner Grayfield and Suzanne Oxenaar and Otto Nan of Seven New Things, founders of Lloyd Hotel & Cultural Embassy (the world’s first 1- to 5-star hotel) and Hotel The Exchange.

neighborhood_overview:
The Jordaan is a famous district in the center of Amsterdam. Originally it was a working-class neighbourhood, but now the Jordaan is one of the most popular and lively districts of Amsterdam. Most of the streets and canals are named after flowers and trees, like Rozenstraat and Rozenstraat(‘’rose’’) or Bloemgracht and Bloemstraat(‘’flower’’). It is a cosy neighbourhood with many traditional cafes, art galleries, modern art and speciality shops and restaurants.

There are 3 markets in this district; the Noordermarkt, the Westerstraat and at the Lindengracht. Around these areas there are a lot of traditional cafes and bars.

neighborhood_overview:
AMSTERDAM – EASTERN ISLANDS

Where water and modern architecture meet
Surrounded by glistening water on all sides, the Oostelijke Eilanden (Eastern Docklands) are a delight to behold. As the Maritime Museum (Scheepvaartmuseum) recalls the sea pioneers of the past, their adventurous spirits live on in its original modern architecture and historic warehouses repurposed into contemporary hotspots.



AMSTERDAM CENTRUM - CITY CENTER + CANAL DISTRICT
In addition to the world-famous historic Canal Ring, Amsterdam's City Centre district ('Centrum') is home to a host of renowned museums, charming and high-end shopping areas, fantastic culture and entertainment.

Amsterdam’s renowned art and culture and top shopping
In Amsterdam’s centre you will, of course, find the magnificent Canal Ring, but there is so much more besides. The inner city, for instance, is bursting with interesting museums, sometimes hidden behind beautiful canal-side faça

name: Stylish ‘North’ house with garden, free P (+cat)
neighborhood_overview:
This is a typical Amsterdam North house in a quiet and cute (dead end) street with lovely ambience. If feels like you are miles away from the buzzin NDSM wharf of the centre, but actually you are really close. Only ten minutes by bike to the hip and upcoming NDSM shipyard with great restaurants and bars, such as Noorderlicht Café and Pllek. Additionally, there is EYE film museum with its great bar-restaurant.
From here you can take the ferries to take you to the central side very easily. Also Twiske (lovely nature park with lots of water and opportunities to swim and spectacular for walking) is walking distance. The new Noth-South Metro line is also close and brings you in 10 min to central station and other sides of town.
On top of that, it is the only area inside the Ring Highway where there is still free parking in the street (great of you come by car, you can then rent a bike close by and take the ferry of the metro to the centre).

neighborhood_overview:
Motoryacht Amstelle is located on a unique and beautiful location called 'Nieuwe Meer'. On the south side the lake borders on the Amsterdamse Bos. On the north side is the nature and recreation area De Oeverlanden. In about 25 minutes you can reach the centre of Amsterdam by public transport. You could say, the best of both worlds, staying in nature on the edge of the bustling city centre. Directly on the harbor is a restaurant with a large terrace that offers a beautiful view over the lake. Within walking distance of the harbor (1500 meters) you can rent bikes. Near the harbor you will find several supermarkets, which you can reach on foot but with the bike you can do your shopping faster.

name: Luxurious bright 200sqm house in city centre!
neighborhood_overview:
The property is located between Brouwersgracht and Rozengracht, near Multatuliplein. The Singel forms part of the famous Amsterdam canals and has now been inscribed on the Unesco World Heritage list. Singel is the innermost (main) canal and is within walking distance of Haarlemmerstraat, Noordermarkt, Dam square and the popular 9 straatjes shopping area. This charming and bustling part of De Jordaan is still very authentic and offers something for everyone: a great variety of bars, restaurants and boutiques, and, if you like to engage in sports, Westerpark is within easy walking distance. This area also offers the opportunity to engage in a variety of cultural activities, for example at the Concertgebouw and the internationally renowned Rijksmuseum, Stedelijk Museum and Van Gogh Museum. You can also find the Anne Frankhuis and the famous Westertoren in de Jordaan nearby.
The area has excellent public transport links (trams and buses) and the A-10, A-2, A-9 and A-4 motorways as wel
host_about: Hi, my name is Neal and I am the proud owner of Neal Estate. I have extensive experience traveling and working all over the world, with my base in Amsterdam. name: Amstel Rembrandtplein
description:
A real home away from home with a separate bedroom, kitchen, 2 bathrooms and a large living with direct views on Amstel River and the Opera building.

The front is on Amstel and from the back you can see buildings on Rembrandtplein. It's in a traditional canal house with wooden floors and high ceilings.

You'll have your own entrance on the third floor, to be reached by stairs, as there is no lift. Total surface of the apt. is 100m2.

7 nights minimum
neighborhood_overview:
This corner of Amstel, next to Blauwbrug, was the historical entrance to the medieval city before the digging of the 17th century canals. Always busy and lively, it evolved into an important cultural and residential neighbourhood with some of the grandest canal houses.

Many cultural institutions are in the direct vicinity, such as Hermitage Museum, the house of Six at Amstel 218 (with Rembrandt's iconic painting of burgomaster Six), Opera/Ballet, Carré Theater and Kleine Komedie.

The best way to get around is on foot or bicycle.

Supermarkets can be found at Rembrandtplein in The Bank building and at Jodenbreestraat (open until 10pm). At Waterlooplein is a branch of ecological food store Ekoplaza. The smaller shops are mostly around Utrechtsestraat.

If you would like more personal recommendations, please feel free to call or text me.
host_about: I love to be able to give people the opportunity to stay in this wonderful property. Traveling around the globe, it always feels great coming back to it. Hope you enjoy it as much as I do.

description:

If you fly into Europe, i'd love it if you'd compensate for your CO2-emisssion. When you let me know you did i will give you a little discount. I dont support flying within Europe. Do consider the train or bus instead. Tell me!
neighborhood_overview:
The East Side of town is becoming more and more a studentfriendly part of town. College and University are expanding on this side of the river Amstel, and coffee bars and cafes are popping up. Our Oosterparkbuurt has a nice feel to it. Outside there is a lot to explore, and the Centre is not far away on bike, with tram or on foot. 'Oost got the most'.

Lots of restaurants and bars and a good park. Best perk: super great organic shop at the corner of our block, Ekodis. And the African food place Kilimanjaro take me Away.

The Beukenplein at the end of our block is a great place to catch a glimpse of the Dutch cafe life.
host_about:
I came to Amsterdam as a student and I worked with youngsters (8 to 19 yrs old) for two decades till 2014 (in Dutch: Ik werkte 20 jaar met jongeren en verliet de jeugdzorg in 2014).

At 44 I started my own Soap business in Januari 2015: De Amsterdamsche Zeepfabriek B.V. The Amsterdam Soap Factory, in Amsterdam. A social firm where we make organic handmade soap bars in Amsterdam, with whoever needs the place to get back to work and to be with others while doing that. Based on 500 years of craftsmanship of soap making as it was big business in Old Amsterdam in 1550.  Soaps & Shampoobars.

Born in Utrecht, in the early seventies, I moved to Amsterdam in '89 to live and study here, and I never left. Except for vacations abroad: California, Australia, London-city and hitchhiking through Europe. Oh, and then New York and Joshua Tree. And Seattle, Austin, Savannah and Santa Monica.. Closer to home I love Vlieland in September, and the Netherlands for camping out. And train and boats to Madrid, BXL, Swiss and Crete..

I was lucky to get a chance to live in this house in the nineties: a small floor just for me with everything I needed. Then two friends came to live with me and we got another floor. And then I got the chance to redo the whole house (2nd, 3rd and 4th floor) with my father Bram and his mate Dion in 2011. The house gets to stay another 100 years!

My house is now the most beautiful loft-house in Amsterdam. A bit hippy. You might want to have a glass with me at the rooftop. I'll think about it ;)

The floor you'll stay on used to be for me and my two bonuschildren: they moved on. I like to use the rooms for guests: come stay, and help me pay off the bank :)

name: NEW! Boutique apartment Eelhouse @Jordaan
description:
Charming and quaint B&B in the historic heart of the 'Jordaan' in Amsterdam's city centre.

Our historic building dates from 1619 and is of typical Amsterdam authentic style with stepped gables.
neighborhood_overview:
The Jordaan is a district in the citycenter of Amsterdam, known for its beautiful houses, nice restaurants and original shops. When in Amsterdam, it is a must to stroll through the little streets and canals. On the many bridges over the canals, you can take beautiful pictures and see why Amsterdam is called the Venice of the North. Popular streets in the Jordaan are the Prinsengracht, the Westerstraat, Haarlemmerstraat and the '9 straatjes' (nine little streets).

In the Jordaan you can visit different types of markets. Every Monday till 13:00 there is a flea market at the Noordermarkt. It is the perfect place to buy old-fashioned Dutch plates, pots and linen. There are also many stalls selling clothes and leather accessories. To find the best treasures, make sure you are early!

For food you can go to the Lindenmarkt at the Lindengracht on Saturdays, or to the market on the Westerstraat on Mondays. Every Saturday there is a biological food market at the Noordermark

neighborhood_overview: In terms of design and development, De Baarsjes is like Oud West: densely populated with few green spaces, but lots of wide streets, squares and facilities. In the 1990s, many neglected buildings were refurbished or replaced by new construction. Mercatorplein was extensively renovated and is the only square completely designed by Berlage that still exists in Amsterdam, thanks to a campaign by residents, which ensured that nothing was demolished. Oud West was a higher priority than De Baarsjes in terms of redevelopment, so De Baarsjes is still lagging in social development by comparison. Even so, it remains a popular neighbourhood with many satisfied residents.

This beautiful apartment can be found in the south of the city to the immediate west of the beautiful Amstelpark. The park is the perfect place to enjoy a picnic and grab some quiet time with a good book under any of the thousands of tall trees. The park also incorporates a restaurant, a playground, some galleries, and a mini golf course known locally as ‘midget golf’. Make your way through the park to discover a beautiful authentic windmill alongside the stunning River Amstel. Why not hire bicycles and cycle across the park emerging alongside the river? You can follow it south to the pretty town of Ouder Amstel. On your way there you will pass through open countryside, experience beautiful wildlife and breathe lots of fresh air. name: Amsterdam Farm Lodge: spacious, suited for groups
description:
Experience this 'off the beaten track' accommodation just on the outskirts of Amsterdam. 'Amsterdam Farm Lodge' is a haven for those seeking a sustainable stay, offering a basic but comfortable and spacious accommodation. Nestled within the innovative urban farming project, 'NoordOogst', the lodge offers an escape from the city's hustle and bustle without straying too far away from its joys.
Please note that we're not located directly in the city center so check our location before booking.
neighborhood_overview:
We are on the site of an awesome urban farming project, you'll find fields with crops, pigs, chickens, a restaurant, a veggie take-away, kids daycare, woodworking, bakery, a beekeeper, a wine yard and a brewery. And plenty of space to play.

The hustling and bustling NDSM wharf with is only ten minutes away by bike, from here the ferry to Central Station departs. Ten minutes biking to the other side you can find nature area 'het Twiske' where you can swim, bike and hike. Only 10 minutes by foot you can discover the hidden nature area of 'Noorder IJplas'. The Zaanse Schans, IJselmeer, beach and 'bollenstreek' are easy to reach.
host_about: We are a couple from Amsterdam and we love to travel. A few years ago we've hosted a wintersport chalet in Austria. We liked it so much that we wanted to open our own B&B. What started out with the idea of a small cabin in the backyard, became much bigger when we got the opportunity of opening the Amsterdam Farm Lodge at the urban farming project of NoordOogst. The Amsterdam Farm Lodge is a project for 10 years and it consists of 7 holiday apartments. Let the adventure begin....

Nice to know: each of the streets is named after a flower.

name: Generator -  Private 4-bed Deluxe Room
description: Enjoy extra space in our Private 4-Bed Deluxe Room at Generator Amsterdam. This room features a versatile bed layout with one bunk (2 beds) and two twin beds, perfect for small groups or families. The private bathroom offers added convenience. Each bunk includes a light, personal shelf, USB charging station, and under-bed lockers. Pillow, duvet, and linen are provided. Towels are available at reception for a small fee. Experience comfort and style in this spacious retreat.
neighborhood_overview: Generator Amsterdam is perfectly placed between Centrum and Oost boroughs, providing views of the Oosterpark’s scenic English garden right next door. From our budget hotel in Amsterdam it couldn’t be easier to get out and about; there’s ample public transport nearby to put you among the museums, music, nightlife and culture on offer in every corner.
host_about: Generator Amsterdam is located in the up-and-coming eastern side of the city, overlooking Oosterpark. This former zoological university building can accommodate 564 guests in 168 twin and quadruple rooms, with en-suite bathrooms and incredible views of the surrounding area. There is also one luxury apartment for six people, with views of the park and a self-catered kitchen. It has flexible and spacious social areas, filled with creative design touches like glass-fronted elevators, a light and airy café and an outdoor terrace. We’ve turned the former lecture hall into an impressive chill-out lounge and bar, while the old library is now a relaxation and private events room. The basement and café are now dining destinations and the original boiler room has become a secret late night bar.

name: Funky ground floor apartment with spacious garden
description:
Beautiful ground floor apartment in a quiet living area with big garden and outside covered sitting area. You will find a modern kitchen with open living room, a newly renovated bathroom and cozy bedroom with kingsize bed inside the apartment. All areas styled in a creative way.
Walking distance to the Kinkerstraat & Vondelpark area and 15 min with public transport to the inner-city areas like the Leidseplein, Jordaan, Dam or any of the Canals. Biking the city would be even quicker ;-)
neighborhood_overview:
Oud-West and in particular De Baarsjes is an upcoming area close to everything the beautiful city of Amsterdam has to offer. It is a 5 minute walk towards the public transport (tram) which takes you within another max of 15 minutes to wherever you wanna go within the city.
If you wanna walk the nearby area you will find both the Vondelpark, as the bustling Kinkerstraat, Jan Pieter Heijestraat & Overtoom within a 5 to 10 minute range.
If you want to feel like a true Amsterdammian or 'Mokummer' as we would say, we would suggest you to rent a pushbike and cruise the city like the rest of us do. You will see that the Jordaan, Damsquare, Leidseplein and Canal area are only a 10 minute bike ride away. There is a good bike rent shop nearby were you can rent bikes for 8,50 euro p/d. A definite must do if possible!

THE WESTERPARK AREA
The area has a varied mix of independent shops, old-school street markets, shiny new restaurants, traditional brown cafés and young expat and Amsterdammer families.

THE WESTERPARK (a 5 minute walk)
The modern and exciting, 14-hectare city park that surrounds the Westergasfabriek buildings is not only a perfect place to relax in natural surroundings, but also a venue for digital art and culture.

THE JORDAAN (a 10 minute walk)
The Jordaan is nothing but highlights. Maze-like streets offer hidden courtyards, cafes (both traditional and trendy), art galleries and pleasantly nosy neighbors. One also has access to some of the more scenic outdoor markets in the country. In particular, Noordermarkt has a flea market on Monday mornings and an organic farmers' market on Saturdays.

neighborhood_overview:
The neighborhood is called Spaarndammerbuurt. A lovely area that used to be working class but has developed a lot over the past decade. It features some great architecture from the beginning of the 20th century of the famous 'Amsterdamse School'. You can also find shops, bars and restaurants on the mean street at a 5 minute walk. Like pretty much all of Amsterdam, the area is very safe and quiet both during the day and night.

name: Sweets hotel Oosterdoksdraaibrug
description: Ever wanted a front-row seat to Amsterdam’s best views without leaving your bath? Designed by the architectural firm Kerste-Meijer, this bridge house combines contemporary style with its historical setting. Perched high above, it offers a unique vantage point with windows all around, making you feel like you’re sailing on a ship. The light-filled space provides stunning 360° views of Amsterdam, from its 17th-century quay to the former naval base.
host_about:
SWEETS hotel is a one-of-a-kind hotel located across Amsterdam in 28 different bridge houses. Each bridge house has been transformed into an independent hotel suite that accommodates a maximum of two guests.

For centuries, Amsterdam’s bridge houses accommodated the city’s many bridge keepers who were responsible for opening the impressive structures for passing water traffic. With building dates between 1673 and 2009, the bridge houses carry an extensive history. Visiting them in chronological order is like a journey through time: their various architectural styles reveal how the city developed and grew, when new quarters were built and even how people thought about city development at that time.

With the hotel room interiors, SWEETS hotel offers guests a unique and comfortable living space, while keeping the history of every bridge house in mind. All bridge houses are different, and so are the interiors. Every SWEET accommodates up to two adults and contains a kingsize double bed, a bathroom and coffee and tea facilities.

SWEETS hotel, first initiated in 2012 as an urban space project, is 8 years in the making. Over half of the 28 bridge houses are currently available for reservations, with more coming soon.

SWEETS hotel is an initiative and co-creation of the architecture office Space&Matter (known for De Ceuvel, an ecological hub), project development partner Grayfield and Suzanne Oxenaar and Otto Nan of Seven New Things, founders of Lloyd Hotel & Cultural Embassy (the world’s first 1- to 5-star hotel) and Hotel The Exchange.

neighborhood_overview: Antwerp is Belgium’s second-largest city, has its biggest port and is the diamond capital of the world. It has long been a powerful magnet for everyone from fashion moguls and club queens, to artists, art lovers and diamond dealers. In the mid-16th century it was one of Europe’s most important cities and home to baroque superstar painters like Pieter Paul Rubens. Antwerp has an intriguing medieval heart with plenty of café-filled cobbled lanes, a riverside fortress and a truly impressive Cathedral. Today, however, Antwerp’s top qualities are its vibrant fashion and entertainment scene, booming diamond businesses along with its startling architectural and cultural contrasts AND amazing restaurants. Antwerp has no less than 17 Michelin-starred restaurants!

neighborhood_overview:
Paardenmarkt, nestled in the heart of Antwerp, Belgium, is an enchanting and historic district.

This picturesque enclave is renowned for its charming cobblestone streets, quaint architectural wonders, and an animated ambiance that effortlessly marries the allure of yesteryears with contemporary vitality.

The district's name, "Horse Market," harkens back to its historical prominence as a trading hub for equines. Today, Paardenmarkt is a thriving neighborhood, replete with inviting cafes, boutique shops, and cultural treasures, rendering it an essential stop for those keen on exploring Antwerp's rich heritage.

With its warm and welcoming atmosphere, Paardenmarkt offers a delightful window into the city's past, all the while remaining an effervescent part of its present.

The apartment is fully equipped and furnished in a japandi design done by an interior designer. The place offers 2 bedrooms, with 55 inch TV in the living room, and a flat screen tv in the masterbedroom.

The best place to stay to explore the city of Antwerp. You are staying in the heart of Antwerp’s old town.

There is a washing machine with its own laundry room, and a lift to access the apartment.

neighborhood_overview:
Located in the Hoogstraat, you are staying in the very center of Antwerps old town. Only a few minutes walking from the famous Cathedral, which was built around 1400.

From " Het Steen " you delve deeper into the Historical Center. First stop: the Grote Markt, the beating heart of the city. Stand still, look around and enjoy the impressive town hall, the centuries-old guild houses and the legendary Brabo Fountain.

When the sun shines you can get a spot on one of the many cozy terraces. Have you ever tasted a Triple d'Anvers at Den Engel? For the perfect caffeine boost, be sure to make a pit stop at Caffe Mundi. Here you can taste delicious coffee, made from freshly roasted coffee beans from neighboring Cross Roast.

Antwerp is of course also known as the diamond city. A visit to the nearby DIVA is therefore indispensable. Find out everything about jewelry, diamonds, gold and silver!

Today, however, Antwerp’s top drawcards are its vibrant fashion and entertainment scene, booming diamond businesses along with its startling architectural and cultural contrasts.

neighborhood_overview: Antwerp is Belgium’s second largest city, has the biggest port and is the capital of coal. It has long been a powerful magnet for everyone from fashion moguls and club queens to art lovers and diamond dealers. In the mid-16th century it was one of Europe’s most important cities and home to baroque superstar painters like Pieter Paul Rubens. Despite many historical travails thereafter and severe WWII bombing, Antwerp retains an intriguing medieval heart with plenty of café-filled cobbled lanes, a riverside fortress and a truly impressive Cathedral. Today, however, Antwerp’s top drawcards are its vibrant fashion and entertainment scene, booming diamond businesses along with its startling architectural and cultural contrasts.

neighborhood_overview: Antwerp is Belgium’s second-largest city, has its biggest port and is the diamond capital of the world. It has long been a powerful magnet for everyone from fashion moguls and club queens, to artists, art lovers and diamond dealers. In the mid-16th century it was one of Europe’s most important cities and home to baroque superstar painters like Pieter Paul Rubens. Antwerp has an intriguing medieval heart with plenty of café-filled cobbled lanes, a riverside fortress and a truly impressive Cathedral. Today, however, Antwerp’s top qualities are its vibrant fashion and entertainment scene, booming diamond businesses along with its startling architectural and cultural contrasts AND amazing restaurants. Antwerp has no less than 17 Michelin-starred restaurants!

name: Rustige kamer Liselle in authentieke meesterwoning
description:
We would like to welcome you to our own house, a master house from 1858, located between the old city center and the trendy Eilandje. Here we offer two rooms with a private bathroom.

In our north-facing room Liselle, you'll enjoy the combination of authentic character, contemporary decor, and modern comfort. The room is located at the back of the house so you can enjoy your stay in peace and quiet.
neighborhood_overview:
Our property is the ideal base if you want to experience Antwerp in all the facets that the city is rich:

WATER: At 300m from our door you can go windsurfing or jogging on the quays along the river Scheldt. The marina and boat trips are also within walking distance

CULTURE: With us you sleep almost "under the tower" of the St. Paul's Church. Take a look here where you can still admire paintings by Rubens, Jordaens,... Furthermore, you can sniff the rich history of Antwerp in the MAS and the old city center at less than 600m.

FOOD & DRINK: In the immediate vicinity you will find numerous terraces to enjoy an Antwerp beer or good food. Around the corner you will find numerous restaurants, bistro and the star restaurant Nathan. We are happy to advise you on your choice.

We do not offer breakfast ourselves, but we are happy to recommend some breakfast restaurants within a 5-minute walk.

SHOPPING: Antwerp is the city of choice for sho
host_about:
Als kind was het altijd mijn droom een eigen hotel uit te baten. Ik verzamelde jarenlang hotelfolders en speelde ook vaak reisbureautje. 

De microbe voor reizen en toerisme was aangewakkerd. 

In de middelbare school en op de Hogeschool ging ik in Brugge toerisme studeren. Uiteindelijk werk ik al enkele jaren in de financiële sector en verhuisde ik voor de liefde naar Antwerpen. Toch is de passie voor reizen en toerisme altijd gebleven.  

Met de aankoop begin 2020  van onze karaktervolle meesterwoning in Antwerpen, zag ik de kans om de draad van toerisme terug op te nemen. Enkele maanden later bieden we met “Upstairs” onze gerenoveerde gastenkamers Camille en Liselle aan om u te ontvangen. 

De authentieke elementen hebben we zoveel mogelijk proberen te bewaren, gecombineerd met een eigentijdse toets. Tegelijk bieden we u graag alle modern comfort van nieuwe privé badkamers met een inloopdouche, lavabo en toilet; Nespresso-apparaat; föhn,...

Voor de gordijnen, stoffering, bed- & badlinnen kozen we voor lokale bedrijven en Belgische kwaliteit. Het is heerlijk om na een dagje Antwerpen in slaap te vallen onder die witte percal katoenen lakens...

name: The Sebastian
description: The Sebastian is a spacious duplex in on original Antwerp Townhouse. With 2 bedrooms it is the ideal house for a short or longer stay for friends or families close to the city center but in one of the most loved neighborhoods of Antwerp, Zurenborg. Situated almost on the dageraadplaats square you will feel in the middle of a city village with a great choice of restaurants and bars. neighborhood_overview: The Sebastian is located in one of the most cozy and buzzing neighborhoods of Antwerp, Zurenborg. The "Dageraadplaats' is just at your doorstep where you can find numerous hip bars, classic café's, great restaurants and a popular playground for the little ones. Zurenborg is also famous for its gorgeous Belle Epoque architecture that is all around you. The shopping district and the old city are just a bike or tram ride away but there is no other place like this 'village in the city' to stay and make your Antwerp experience complete.

neighborhood_overview:
Explore Antwerp: A Fusion of Historical Charm and Modern Vibrancy

In the heart of Flanders, Antwerp is a city where history and modernity intertwine, creating a neighborhood rich in culture and contemporary allure. Wander through cobblestone streets lined with medieval architecture, highlighted by the Gothic Cathedral of Our Lady, showcasing Peter Paul Rubens' masterpieces, as well as going though the lively Grote Markt, that with its ornate guild houses, offers a glimpse into the city's vibrant past.

Antwerp's status as a fashion capital is evident in its trendy Fashion District, where chic boutiques and avant-garde designers reflect the city's stylish spirit. The Royal Academy of Fine Arts further adds to this fashionable ambiance. The city's parks and the Scheldt riverbanks provide tranquil green spaces, offering a peaceful escape from urban life.

The old city centre is steeped in history. You will find it in ancient building-fronts on narrow streets or in the imposing Grand-Place. The Plantin-Moretus Museum is the only museum in the world to be classified as a Unesco World Heritage site. In the shadow of the Cathedral of Our Lady, the city teems with life in intimate pubs and restaurants. The banks of the Scheldt are a great place for a breath of fresh air.

Antwerp is a world city on a human scale. There’s always something to see and experience from interesting museums and fun attractions to surprising spots and architectural highlights.

"Finger-licking good food? Antwerp is the place to be! Experience the city's welcoming terraces on the many squares and, in addition to Belgian staples such as fries and chocolate, sample local delicacies such as the beer "Bolleke" and the liqueur "Elixir d'Anvers".

neighborhood_overview: Zurenborg is a picturesque and lively neighbourhood. It is a unique and protected area in Antwerp, which is famous for its Art Nouveau, Neo-Gothic and Neo-Renaissance architecture. It is full of little bars and restaurants, and is at only 10 minutes on foot from the train station Antwerpen-Berchem. The car-free square "Dageraadplaats" and the square "Draakplaats" are the animated centers of the neighbourhood, lined with bars and restaurants that are always full of local people who sip a drink while keeping an eye on their children playing in the square's playground.

name: 2 bedroom apartment |Trendy Antwerp-South
description:
Welcome to our cozy private apartment located in the back of our renovated townhouse in the famous Antwerp-Zuid area.

Our house is in a quiet dead end street but a short walk away from the trendy and exciting city center with its bars, musea and shops.

This place is perfect for a romantic weekend getaway, a city trip with friends or for a longer stay (contact us now for special offer).
neighborhood_overview:
It's a young and family-oriented neighbourhood. Not touristy at all: right across from our door you have cool coffee bar Copper, owned by our neighbour, and exquisite vegan restaurant And/or. A new and hidden gem, at the moment only locals have discovered it! There are many supermarkets and nice little bars, as well as park Hof Van Leysen.
But don't be mistaken: the hustle and bustle of Antwerp-Zuid is right nearby. In under ten minutes walking you will find yourself at the steps of the world-class, newly renovated Museum of Fine Arts (MUHKA) at Museumplein. The area around it (nicknamed Petit Paris) is famous for its little squares with winebars, art galeries and many, many shops. You could walk around there for days and still not get bored.
Just a few more streets ahead you arrive at the bank of the river Schelde, good for long, introspective walks and sightseeing all the unique architectural riverside appartments. It's Antwerp at it finest: classic grandeur meets flashy

description: Experience the charm of Antwerp’s city center in this charming apartment, located in the historic heart of Old Town Antwerp. Stroll down the famous Meir shopping street, soak up the culture in one of our many museums or have a drink or bite in one of the countless bars or restaurants. Located within walking distance of the central station and all other main tourist attractions, you’ll be able to explore everything Belgium's second-largest city has to offer first hand. Hope to see you soon!
neighborhood_overview: Antwerp is Belgium’s second-largest city, has its biggest port and is the diamond capital of the world. It has long been a powerful magnet for everyone from fashion moguls and club queens, to artists, art lovers and diamond dealers. In the mid-16th century it was one of Europe’s most important cities and home to baroque superstar painters like Pieter Paul Rubens. Antwerp has an intriguing medieval heart with plenty of café-filled cobbled lanes, a riverside fortress and a truly impressive Cathedral. Today, however, Antwerp’s top qualities are its vibrant fashion and entertainment scene, booming diamond businesses along with its startling architectural and cultural contrasts AND amazing restaurants. Antwerp has no less than 17 Michelin-starred restaurants!

name: Asheville Overlook! Comfy 2br Asheville Apt/House!
description: This spacious retreat is conveniently located in Asheville, just minutes to Downtown! Pop over to River Arts District in a few minutes or drive 15 minutes south to Asheville and enjoy the breweries! Feel at home with the strong wifi, modern furniture, and extra amenities. This listing is perfect for a friends or families looking for a weekend getaway. neighborhood_overview:
This home is nestled in a local neighborhood and farming community just 1.5 miles from the French Broad River, the main waterway through the Blue Ridge Mountains. Enjoy the water activities (including fly fishing, white water rafting, floating in a tube or just hanging near the water) or use this great location to access the River Arts District only 5 minutes away! River Arts District is a working art studios and galleries of hundreds of artists.

Within 10-15 minutes, you will find yourself in the heart of downtown Asheville. Stop along the way to the Botanical Gardens or enjoy a nice pint of beer or cup of coffee!
host_about:
We are pleased to welcome you to Hopetown Homes!

After spending four years together at college in Maine, the three of us became busy with jobs and life. We remained best friends since we were 18 years old but wanted to make sure that we could always make time to see one another and spend quality time together. Over the past decade, the Asheville area – including the very properties that you will stay at – offered us the opportunity to reconnect, build bonds and share our lives together during major life events (career changes, girlfriends, marriage and now kids!). 

Thank you for visiting our page and spending time at our home. We hope that you too experience the same camaraderie, reconnection and joy that these homes have given us! Please let us know what we can do to make your stay as enjoyable as possible - we love the local area and want to share it with you!

-Keegan, Bryan, Bernie

name: Cozy Haw Creek Retreat
description: Located in Haw Creek, in convenient East Asheville. We are 4.1 miles from downtown and 2.1 miles from the Blue Ridge Parkway. This private guest suite is in a quiet neighborhood surrounded by trees and wildlife. The renovated space was completed in late 2021 and has its own private entrance, deck, and hot tub.
neighborhood_overview:
Located in East Asheville in the 28805 zip code, Haw Creek was named for the native Black Haw shrub or small tree (Viburnum prunifolium) that grew more abundantly years ago along the moist soils of the valley, especially along Haw Creek itself.

Haw Creek valley’s southern boundary extends from US Highway 70/Tunnel Road at I-240 eastward along Tunnel Road to Maple Springs Road. Its west boundary is formed by Piney Mountain/Bear Mountain ridge; Town Mountain creates the northern boundary; and the Blue Ridge Parkway stretches along most of its eastern border.

Haw Creek was settled in the early 1800’s as a farming community with the first school established in 1842 on the site of the present day Bethesda Cemetery. Haw Creek has two main entrances with very narrow roads throughout the valley. It is one of the few communities that does not have through traffic to other areas of town, resulting in a quieter neighborhood than most. With vehicle access into and out of th

During my husband and my renovation of our new home in East Asheville we decided to include the perfect little cozy suite so that we can share our love of the City with others.  It sits nestled on the side of our home in a quiet little setting.

He and I love to travel and have utilized Airbnb in the past and thought it as the perfect next step in the process.

We have lived and worked in Asheville for just about 20 years. Eric works as an architect downtown and he tries to ride his road and mountain bikes as much as he can. I am a school teacher and you might run into me heading out for a walk with our new puppy, Rue.

name: Cumberland Cottage, Chef's Kitchen, Walk to Town
description: This 1st floor, furnished monthly rental has everything you need to feel right at home. The open concept and spacious one bedroom unit comes equipped with a private entrance, chef's kitchen, and a luxury master bath with walk in shower. The unit offers filtered water from every fixture, on site parking steps from the door, comfortable furniture, full size appliances including a dishwasher, a 56" flat screen TV, dedicated 500 MPS WiFi, and a mini split for your heating and cooling needs.
neighborhood_overview:
This comfortable and convenient unit is perfectly situated in "Montford", a historic tree lined district which is one of the most desirable neighborhoods to live and stay in. Just north of downtown, this is a mature, residential area close to shopping, restaurants, and entertainment and was developed by the architects, engineers and craftspeople who built the "Biltmore". The original brick laid sidewalks are ideal for taking leisurely strolls to admire the architecture, visit with welcoming neighbors, or venture into town. The entire neighborhood boasts large trees and a park every couple of blocks, the closest of which is across the street and has tennis, pickleball, and basketball courts.
Several nearby eateries offer a variety of menus and libations within walking distance. Surrounded by trees and charming homes, you'll love how close you are to downtown while feeling tucked away in your own cozy paradise.
host_about: I started living at my property as a tenant and found a creative way to buy and renovate it without any money or experience. This sanctuary is perfect for couples, solo adventurers, and business travelers needing a private place to relax, work, reflect, and play.

The apartment is in a quiet, lovely historic neighborhood located slightly north of downtown Asheville.

name: Rose Ranch Montford 1, central, private suite
description: Your room, light and cozy, with a private entrance and private bath, is part of our owner occupied home. Can be rented in conjunction with "Rose Ranch Montford 2," allowing up to 2 additional guests. In Montford,  20 minute brisk, 30 minute leisurely, to downtown. Wonderful, friendly, diverse neighborhood, 2 blocks from the Historic Riverside Cemetery. Many trees and Victorian homes. Great for walking, running, biking and getting exercise!
neighborhood_overview: Quiet shady neighborhood, with lots of kids and diversity. Our neighborhood is a mixture of ranch style homes, Victorian homes and cute bungalows. Great for running, walking and biking. An abundance of trees and flowers.
host_about:
Born in NC, attended UNC-Chapel Hill and got a degree in Journalism. My mom's side of the family has lived in Western North Carolina for the past century and a half. I have relatives buried in the Riverside Cemetery, which we can see from our backyard.
However, I lived most of my adult life on the West Coast. After college, I drove west in a school bus and eventually landed outside Eugene, OR, next to a creek, grew lots of my food, and had a sewing business, selling at the Saturday Market, Circle of Hands and craft fairs up and down the West Coast.
I have been to all 50 states, Canada, several Central American countries, Caribbean islands and Europe. I LOVE meeting people from all around the world and have studied conflict resolution in large groups of people using World Work. I am a therapist full time, offering massage therapy as well as Bodywisdom Therapy, helping people unravel the messages of their bodies, at a location on Merrimon Avenue. I speak a bit of French and Spanish, but am alas, no longer fluent.
My son and I moved back to WNC several years ago to be near my Mom, who lives in Yancey County. I know some sweet areas to visit around the area and are including them in a notebook in the rooms. 
I love being able to offer people a centrally located place to enjoy what WNC has to offer, as well as a prime walking neighborhood to have access to downtown Asheville hotspots. 
I turned both bedrooms and bathrooms downstairs into places that I would like to visit myself, using art from my own collection, and textures and colors that I love.
We have friends and family over on a regular basis so there is a bit of hustle and bustle at times. If you have children, or like to come and go and appreciate the outdoors, you will fit right in! If you want privacy, the private entrance and keypad will fit your needs. If you want total peace and quiet, I am afraid we won't be your cup of tea.
Living lightly on the earth is a core value for me so we recycle as much as possible, and keep our throwaway consumables to a minimum. 
Life is for loving, sharing and making the world better wherever we can.

host_about:
Hello! My husband and I are looking forward to hosting guests in our home as well as being a guest of others during our travels. We became AirBnB hosts in 2014 and have since hosted more than 2,000 bookings. In 2018, we became AirBnB superhosts!

We live in Asheville in the beautiful mountains of western North Carolina.

Allison works as a fourth grade teacher at Mills River Elementary School. Peter works as the Trails Coordinator at a regional land trust, Conserving Carolina. Both of us work to keep this region we love as wonderful as it is today, and make it even better for future generations.

We love to take road trips throughout the year. We have appreciated the kindness of many over the years who have let us stay in their home or camp in their yard, or have rented a room to us for a reasonable rate. We are guests who are focused on our travels and we most often are seeking simply a room to hang our hat for the night before our travels resume the following morning. For that reason, most hosts barely know that we are there. In appreciation for the kindness shown to us over the years, we pay it forward by being clean and courteous when we are guests in the homes of others, and we are seeking to host guests who are similarly self-sufficient and simply seeking a basic place for the night.

We look forward to you staying in our home!

This neighborhood is called Westwood Place. It is a quiet, charming city spot with a mix of traditional and modern trendy family homes. You can walk the beautiful neighborhood, and it is a pleasant stroll to the River Arts District, and to downtown West Asheville.
West Asheville and River Arts District are the most eclectic, interesting, and unique areas in Asheville. My Homestay is at an unbeatable location if you want to be close to the three main city urban centers (River Arts District, West Asheville downtown, Asheville downtown) -A few distances below:

-Downtown West Asheville, 3 minutes drive, 15 minutes walk (amazing eclectic restaurants, cafes, shops....)
-River Arts District 4 minutes drive, 18 minutes walk ( several wonderful artists workshops and expositions, places to go for food and drink-I will make a list of recommendations for my guests)

host_about:
I spent my entire adult life in theatre as an actress, director and producer. Nearly 20 years of that I created original works with objects and puppets. My work has been seen at The Kennedy Center, the Henson International Festival in N.Y., UCLA, and the Walker Arts Center, to name a few.  I tired of the touring life however and am now making beautiful handmade art lamps inspired by a 2 year stay in Korea.  I love my hours in the studio which is located in Asheville's River Arts District!  I must still travel some to get my wares out into the public, but with a studio in the district, many travelers come my way. 

I love to meet new people. I'm friendly and easy to get along with.  With all the traveling done over the years I am quite self sufficient.  Good food is important when you're on the road, and I love to find those special little places tucked away in a neighborhood. As a host, I will be able to point you in the right direction for a wonderful night on the town. 

My motto is - "Be positive and enjoy the ride."

An outdoor amphitheater at Pack Square features local cloggers during the summer evenings and Friday night drum circle around the corner at Pritchard Park is a must! Eat at great restaurants, shop our markets and just let yourself get lost canvasing the winding city streets and parks. We are a two minute walk to Harrah's Cherokee Event Center and a quick five minute drive to the Biltmore Estate or the Grove Park Inn. Want a day away from the city? Enjoy the Blue Ridge Parkway for awe inspiring views, go for a hike and stretch your legs; Asheville has it all!

Private entrance and located on a quiet, residential street (you can hear the crickets at night). The studio offers everything you'll need for a comfortable stay: queen bed, new bath, plush sofa, table for two, and other thoughtful amenities.

Your pup will be allowed upon approval. A $75.00 non refundable pet fee will be applied after approval.
neighborhood_overview:
The studio is in West Asheville, an artsy, hip part of the city. It's a short walk to the neighborhood's main hub, Haywood Road, where you'll find numerous coffee shops, eateries, breweries, and live music venues.

UpCountry Brewing and Izzy's Coffee House are a 10-minute walk. Other renowned West Asheville spots:
Biscuit Head: 1.2 miles (by foot/car)
Sunny Point Cafe: 1.5 miles (by foot/car)
The Admiral: 2 miles (by foot/car)
New Belgium Brewery: 2 miles (by car)

The River Arts District, South Slope breweries, and central downtown are all less than a 15-minute drive. The nearest Blue Ridge Parkway entrance is just 7 miles away.

name: Private Guest House! Walkable West Asheville
description: RARE FIND! Grandfathered in from the city ban on detached Airbnbs, our place is a unique! Total privacy. No more trying to sleep in someone's basement while your hosts crash around upstairs. In the neighborhood of West Asheville, our private detached guest house is a short walk to restaurants, music, breweries and shopping.  7min drive to downtown. 500 sqft Three separate rooms. Queen bedroom, full bathroom and separate living room with convertible sofa. Sorry NO PETS.
neighborhood_overview:
Come masquerade as a local and enjoy the short walk to the dozens of popular dining, music, shopping and people watching activities Haywood Rd. has to offer. Though quite close to downtown, 100 years ago West Asheville was its own town independent of Asheville. As a result, this unique area of town has its own “Mini-Downtown” that is centered around Haywood Rd. For the last few decades this commercial corridor has enjoyed a period of reinvestment by the community and area businesses. Several cool and unique restaurants, coffee shops, bars, bakeries, breweries and shops have chosen West Asheville as their home. Century old houses are being renovated back to life and new homes are being built on every available lot. As the historic town of “West Asheville” was never very big, the entire area is very walkable and there are lots of cool old buildings, churches, trees and houses.
The River Arts District and New Belgium Brewing are a 5 minute drive, Biltmore Estate is a 15 minute drive
