name: Convenience, Charm and Comfort
description: You'll love this spacious and airy apartment nestled in the historic tree lined Montford District. Delight in eclectic sun filled rooms with old world charm. This one bedroom, fully outfitted rental  has been recently, lovingly, and completely remodeled. You will find that you never felt more at home while traveling...
neighborhood_overview:
This home is perfectly situated in "Montford", a historic tree lined district which is one of the most desirable neighborhoods to live and stay in. Just a half mile from downtown, this is a mature, residential area close to shopping, restaurants, and entertainment and was developed by the architects, engineers and craftspeople who built the "Biltmore". The original brick laid sidewalks are ideal for taking leisurely strolls to admire the architecture, visit with welcoming neighbors, or venture into town. The entire neighborhood boasts large trees and a park every couple of blocks, the closest of which is across the street and has tennis, pickleball, and basketball courts.
Several nearby eateries offer a variety of menus and libations within walking distance.
The whole city, including the River Arts District, downtown, the South Slope, East side, and "West Asheville" are a mere 3-7 minute drive from your accommodations.
host_about: I started living at my property as a tenant and found a creative way to buy and renovate it without any money or experience. I have several interests which span many disciplines including Illustration, education, history, travel, civic engagement, and the arts. I spent many years in the service industry and understand what it takes to provide an excellent experience for the guest. A spirit of grace and generosity always wins friends and allies...

description:
Welcome to Realtor & Biz Owner Kitty Savage’s private basement apartment in Asheville, designed for music enthusiasts, free spirits, and hemp-friendly visitors.

This 800 sq ft space offers its own driveway, mini fridge, convection oven, large sink, and Keurig coffee maker, a standup shower. A king-size microfiber bed.

host_about:
fantastic location just a few minutes from downtown or Biltmore Village. You'll enjoy an oversized bedroom, with its own full bathroom and entry from the new deck, and amazingly comfy microfiber bedding. 

As a business owner (realtor, media company owner, teeth whitening service), former local music promoter and as a foodie having lived here on and off for ten years, I can tell you about the best spots for your bohemian adventure in Asheville. Like a DJ, I will try to feel you out and see what you may enjoy the most for your particular adventure. 

Sometimes I can host more actively, and sometimes I'm swamped with other plans. I respect my guest's privacy and do not enter their rooms while rented. Mainly I like to check in when you're coming and going, but sometimes I end up making fun friends and going out to events together. So far, everyone who has visited has been a kind soul looking to experience the best that Asheville has to offer, and has been comfortable and satisfied with their experience. I love this little city so much, it is my joy to accommodate liberal minded travelers with a relaxed environment and convenient location.

host_about:
While these “describe yourself” paragraphs are always awkward, I will do my best.
Marcia and I have been married and living in Black Mountain for the last 37 years. We raised two daughters, both of whom have “flown the nest AND the wallet!” We live on a small farm where we raise llamas, horses, alpacas, dogs, arrogant cat, guinea hens, rainbow trout, and the occasional wild fox, coyote, etc.
My wife is a practicing Veterinarian and I have done small real estate developments for most of my career. I love to read and wish I could write better. I currently spend time volunteering with Big Brothers, Big Sisters and other local environmental concerns.
If you like experiencing nature, you may want to check out our Treehouse Glamping Decks near Asheville. Quiet places of retreat on a covered deck with impressively massive views, open aired and rustic, yet lights and electricity for any plug in device you might want. Nearby parking and private toilet facilities.
Our places have been described as well located, of good value, and wonderfully unique. Please read our reviews for more information and an honest evaluation of our hosting ethic. We continue to seek improvement in the experience of our quests. 

We look forward to your instant book reservation,  or hearing your inquiry. 
Doug & Marcia

description:
Asheville welcomes you back after Hurricane Helene!

We are are centrally located for a variety of music
and outdoor activities.

neighborhood_overview:
There are quite a few places to eat/drink within a few blocks of the studio as well as botanical gardens, greenways and parks. Here are just a few suggestions:

North Asheville Tailgate Market (across the street; go to cul-de-sac and walk up the step). Every Saturday, 7 am – 12 pm.

Botanical Gardens: Go to W.T. Weaver Blvd. and turn right on Greenway. Just two blocks away.

Downtown is @ 1.5 miles from the studio, the best way to walk/ride to downtown is to go to right on on the Greenway and follow to end.

Salvage Station (near Studio on Riverside Drive, outdoor venue on river with great food, beer and music).

Luella’s BBQ: great food, including BBQ tempeh, a variety of local beers on tap, go to W.T. Weaver Blvd., take a left on Greenway and walk to the end.

The Hop: walk to the ice cream shop. This is on Merrimon Avenue and you will need to go upstairs to the outside fence gate, go up the driveway, turn left on B

name: Mountain location. Five minutes to downtown
description:
This charming, cozy mid-century modern home is located just 1 mile from downtown and 5 minutes from the entrance to the Biltmore Estate. Close to everything, centrally located on a quiet, forested mountain road with privacy and wildlife! Shared drive and yard with downstairs apartmen

It is the perfect home base to explore Asheville. This is a unique property in the city. Enjoy a mountain escape within the heart of Asheville. We are just 10 minutes to the River Arts District and West Asheville.
neighborhood_overview:
Located on a low mountain between downtown and Tunnel Rd. the home is minutes from Biltmore, West Asheville, the River Arts District, and South Slope breweries. Breakfast opportunities abound, as well as a major supermarket, all less than 3 minutes from the house.

Downtown Asheville is just a 5-minute drive. You can walk over and down the mountain to town in about 25 minutes on a narrow, quiet road and then Uber home for around $7. West Asheville and the River Arts District are a 10-minute drive.

The Biltmore Estate and Biltmore village both neighbor the Kenilworth neighborhood and are just 2 miles away and about a 5-minute drive.

Windswept Drive is just up the hill offering some of the best views of the city found anywhere. There's also an access point to Beaucatcher Mtn Park, a mature forested oasis just above the city. It's accessible from the house for walkers or runners who don't mind climbing, otherwise, there's parking at the trailhead.
host_about:
Entrepreneur, business owner, Caribbean charter boat captain and sailor. I've spent my life making people happy on vacation, from the Mountains of Colorado to the US Virgin Islands. I've always loved welcoming visitors to awesome travel destinations that happen to be my home. 

Asheville is a wonderful small city. I like coffee, bookstores, farmers markets and bicycles. I also love great restaurants and outdoor activities. No wonder I moved to Asheville. 

As a host I like to completely disappear unless I'm needed or wanted, then I'm here to help, offer local tips, make reservations, arrange transportation or whatever you need.
